candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: All of the stars of the Big Dipper may appear to be the same distance from the earth, but in fact they are very far from each other. figure shows the distances from the earth to each of these stars.The distances are given in light-years (ly), the distance that light travels in one year. One light-year equals $9.461 \times 10^{15} m$.To an inhabitant of a planet orbiting Merak. how many degrees apart in the sky would Alkaid and our sun be?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集更可能以单解题为主，不强推多解 agent，只保留基础可验证性与可标注性检查。

normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: All of the stars of the Big Dipper may appear to be the same distance from Earth, but in fact they are very far from each other. The figure shows the distances from Earth to each of these stars. The distances are given in light-years (ly), the distance that light travels in one year. One light-year equals $9.461 \times 10^{15} m$. To an inhabitant of a planet orbiting Merak, how many degrees apart in the sky would Alkaid and our Sun be?

sample_understanding_summary.rationale: The text clearly asks for the angular separation between Alkaid and the Sun as seen from Merak, so the question intent and expected answer type are understandable. However, the referenced figure with the stars/distances is required and is not available here, so the sample cannot be reliably understood or solved jointly. The provided answer string also appears OCR-corrupted.

reject_records.reject_reason_detail: The prompt is already phrased as a direct open-ended question, so it can be kept as-is while removing the multiple-choice options.
